reasoning: Diethyl sebacate is the diethyl ester of sebacic acid (decanedioic acid), making it a dicarboxylic acid diester. It consists of two ethyl ester groups on a 10-carbon chain, classifying it as a fatty acid ester and more specifically a dialkyl ester of a dicarboxylic acid. The most fitting direct parent from the available labels is 'Dialkyl ethers' is incorrect; the best available label is 'Fatty acid esters'.
